sentence: Hoje não vou sair, o tempo está mesmo mau.
ipa: ˈwɔʒɨ ˈnɐ̃w̃ ˈbow ˈsajɾ o ˈtẽpu eˈʃta ˈmjɛʃmu ˈmaw
gloss_en: Today I am not going out, the weather is really bad.
notes: Northern betacism 'vou' realised [bow], natively attested per pwl_braga; open tonic diphthong [jɛ] in 'mesmo' per brissos2018.

sentence: Aquele casaco verde custa quarenta e dois euros.
ipa: ɐˈkjɛlɨ kɐˈzaku ˈbjɛɾdɨ ˈkuʃtɐ kwɐˈɾẽtɐ i ˈdojz ˈewɾuʃ
gloss_en: That green coat costs forty-two euros.
notes: Betacism 'verde' [ˈbjɛɾdɨ] per cintra1971; open tonic diphthong [jɛ] in 'aquele' per brissos2018; coda [ʃ] chiado inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Tenho consulta com o médico às onze desta semana.
ipa: ˈtjɛɲu kõˈsuɫtɐ kõ o ˈmjɛdiku aʃ ˈwõzɨ ˈdjɛʃtɐ sɨˈmanɐ
gloss_en: I have a doctor's appointment this week at eleven.
notes: Tonic diphthong [jɛ] with palatal [ɲ] in 'tenho' per brissos2018; the diphthongisation isogloss per cintra1971; dark coda [ɫ] in 'consulta' inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O meu avô vive numa quinta ali ao pé do rio.
ipa: o ˈmew ɐˈbwo ˈbibɨ ˈnumɐ ˈkĩtɐ ɐˈli ɐu ˈpjɛ du ˈʁiu
gloss_en: My grandfather lives on a farm right by the river.
notes: Betacism 'vive' [ˈbibɨ] per pwl_braga; close [o] diphthong 'avô' [ɐˈbwo] per cintra1971; strong [ʁ] in 'rio' per segura2013.

sentence: Vai por aquela estrada e vira logo à direita.
ipa: ˈbaj puɾ ɐˈkjɛlɐ eˈʃtɾadɐ i ˈbiɾɐ ˈlwɔɡu a diˈɾejtɐ
gloss_en: Go along that road and turn right straight away.
notes: Betacism imperatives 'vai'/'vira' [ˈbaj]/[ˈbiɾɐ] per pwl_braga; 'ei' retention in 'direita' [ej] per cintra1971; 'aquela estrada' liaison inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O caldo verde com broa estava mesmo bom, ó pá.
ipa: o ˈkaɫdu ˈbjɛɾdɨ kõ ˈbɾwɔɐ eˈʃtabɐ ˈmjɛʃmu ˈbõ ˈwɔ ˈpa
gloss_en: The kale soup with cornbread was really good, mate.
notes: Betacism 'verde'/'estava' [b] per cintra1971; dark coda [ɫ] in 'caldo' inherited per mateus_dandrade2000; open diphthong [wɔ] in the interjection 'ó' per brissos2018.

sentence: Onde é que eu vou buscar o comboio para Guimarães?
ipa: ˈwõdɨ ˈjɛ kɨ ˈew ˈbow buˈʃkaɾ o kõˈboju ˈpaɾɐ ɡimɐˈɾɐ̃j̃ʃ
gloss_en: Where do I catch the train to Guimarães?
notes: Betacism 'vou' [bow] and 'ou' preserved [ow], natively attested per pwl_braga; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃j̃] in 'Guimarães' inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Quantos ovos é que levas para o bolo?
ipa: kwɐ̃ˈtuʃ ˈwobuʃ ˈjɛ kɨ ˈljɛbɐʃ ˈpaɾɐ o ˈbwɔlu
gloss_en: How many eggs are you taking for the cake?
notes: Close [o] diphthong with betacism 'ovos' [ˈwobuʃ] and open [wɔ] 'bolo' per cintra1971; open diphthong [jɛ] with betacism in 'levas' per brissos2018.

sentence: Se eu tivesse tempo, ia contigo ver o desfile.
ipa: sɨ ˈew tiˈbjɛs̺ɨ ˈtẽpu ˈiɐ kõˈtiɡu ˈbjɛɾ o dɨˈʃfilɨ
gloss_en: If I had time, I would go with you to see the parade.
notes: Betacism 'tivesse'/'ver' [tiˈbjɛs̺ɨ]/[ˈbjɛɾ] per cintra1971; open diphthong [jɛ] in 'tivesse' per brissos2018; 'se eu' liaison inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Ontem levei o meu neto a passear pela vila velha.
ipa: ˈwõtɨ̃ lɨˈbej o ˈmew ˈnjɛtu ɐ pɐs̺ɨˈaɾ pɨlɐ ˈbilɐ ˈbjɛʎɐ
gloss_en: Yesterday I took my grandson for a walk through the old town.
notes: Betacism 'levei'/'vila'/'velha' [b] per pwl_braga; 'ei' retention 'levei' [lɨˈbej] per cintra1971; palatal [ʎ] in 'velha' inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Para o mês que vem vou de viagem até ao Gerês.
ipa: ˈpaɾɐ o ˈmjeʃ kɨ ˈbẽ ˈbow dɨ biˈaʒɨ̃ ɐˈtjɛ ɐu ʒɨˈɾjeʃ
gloss_en: Next month I am travelling to Gerês.
notes: Betacism 'vou' [bow], natively attested per pwl_braga; close [e] diphthong 'mês' [ˈmjeʃ] and 'Gerês' [ɡɨˈɾjeʃ] per cintra1971.

sentence: Liga-me quando saíres do trabalho, está bem?
ipa: ˈliɡɐ mɨ ˈkwɐ̃du sɐˈiɾɨʒ du tɾɐˈbaʎu eˈʃta ˈbẽ
gloss_en: Call me when you leave work, alright?
notes: Palatal [ʎ] in 'trabalho' inherited per mateus_dandrade2000; 'saíres do' voiced coda-s sandhi [z] inherited per mateus_dandrade2000; north-western reduction to [ɨ] per segura2013.

sentence: Olá, então tu por cá, o que é que contas de novo?
ipa: oˈla ẽˈtɐ̃w̃ ˈtu puɾ ˈka o kɨ ˈjɛ kɨ ˈkõtɐʒ dɨ ˈnwɔbu
gloss_en: Hi, so you are around, what's new with you?
notes: Betacism with open diphthong 'novo' [ˈnwɔbu] per cintra1971; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'então' inherited per mateus_dandrade2000; conversational register per pwl_braga.

sentence: Quem não arrisca, não petisca, dizia a minha avó.
ipa: ˈkɨ̃ ˈnɐ̃w̃ ɐˈʁiʃkɐ ˈnɐ̃w̃ pɨˈtiʃkɐ diˈziɐ ɐ ˈmiɲɐ ɐˈbwɔ
gloss_en: Nothing ventured, nothing gained, my grandmother used to say.
notes: Strong [ʁ] for ⟨rr⟩ in 'arrisca' per segura2013; palatal [ɲ] in 'minha' inherited per mateus_dandrade2000; betacism with open diphthong 'avó' [ɐˈbwɔ] per cintra1971.

sentence: O rio corre veloz por entre as serras do norte.
ipa: o ˈʁiu ˈkwɔʁɨ bɨˈlwɔʃ puɾ ˈjẽtɾɨ ɐʃ ˈsjɛʁɐʒ du ˈnwɔɾtɨ
gloss_en: The river runs swiftly between the northern mountains.
notes: Strong [ʁ] for ⟨rr⟩ in 'corre'/'serras' per segura2013; betacism 'veloz' [bɨˈlwɔʃ] per cintra1971; open diphthong [wɔ] in 'corre'/'norte' per brissos2018.

sentence: Choveu a noite inteira e depois veio o nevoeiro.
ipa: ʃuˈbew ɐ ˈnojt ĩˈtejɾɐ i dɨˈpojʒ ˈbeju o nɨbuˈejɾu
gloss_en: It rained all night and then the fog came in.
notes: Betacism 'choveu'/'veio' [ʃuˈbew]/[ˈbeju] per cintra1971; 'ei' retention 'inteira'/'nevoeiro' [ej] per cintra1971; inherited coda [ʃ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Vês aquele moinho velho lá no cimo do monte?
ipa: ˈbjez ɐˈkjɛlɨ ˈmojɲu ˈbjɛʎu ˈla nu ˈsimu du ˈmõtɨ
gloss_en: Do you see that old mill up on the hilltop?
notes: Betacism with close [e] diphthong 'vês' [ˈbjez] per cintra1971; palatal [ɲ] in 'moinho' and [ʎ] in 'velho' inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Trouxeste o dinheiro todo ou ficou algum em casa?
ipa: tɾoˈwʃjɛʃt o diˈɲejɾu ˈtwɔdu ˈow fiˈkow ɐˈɫɡũ ẽ ˈkazɐ
gloss_en: Did you bring all the money or did some stay at home?
notes: 'ou' retention in 'ou'/'ficou' [ow], natively attested per pwl_braga; dark coda [ɫ] in 'algum' and palatal [ɲ] in 'dinheiro' inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O sino da igreja velha toca sempre ao meio-dia.
ipa: o ˈsinu dɐ iˈɡɾjɛʒɐ ˈbjɛʎɐ ˈtwɔkɐ ˈsẽpɾɨ ɐu ˈmeju ˈdiɐ
gloss_en: The old church bell always rings at midday.
notes: Betacism 'velha' [ˈbjɛʎɐ] with palatal [ʎ] per cintra1971; open diphthong [wɔ] in 'toca' per brissos2018; 'ei' in 'meio' [ej] per cintra1971.

sentence: O sal e o azeite estão na prateleira de cima.
ipa: o ˈsaɫ i o ɐˈzejt eˈʃtɐ̃w̃ nɐ pɾɐtɨˈlejɾɐ dɨ ˈsimɐ
gloss_en: The salt and the oil are on the top shelf.
notes: Dark coda [ɫ] in 'sal' and nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'estão' inherited per mateus_dandrade2000; 'ei' retention 'azeite'/'prateleira' [ej] per cintra1971.
